_comment: Signature DATA the watchdog classifies against (spec Section 2 taxonomy + Section 9.3). This is the part that rots at the speed new loop classes appear (6 signatures in May -> F1-F13 in June -> ~19 classes in July), so it is updated ONLY through the repo + sanctioned rollout - never hand-edited on a box. NO secret values live here; the anthropic-family entries are DENY DATA, and secret detection is delegated to scan-no-secrets.sh by version pin.

loop_families.A: AGENT-TURN LOOPS (a model call inside the loop - the token burners)
loop_families.B: PROCESS / SUPERVISOR LOOPS (restart storms; churn + outage, no model call)
loop_families.D: TASK / PROGRESS LOOPS (stalls that look healthy)

secret_shapes.note: The secret-CLASS detector is reused verbatim. This pin records which classes the watchdog expects; a class version bump rides the repo, never a box-local edit. The VALUE is never stored, printed, or reproduced.

anthropic_family_deny_note: The watchdog makes ZERO model calls, so this rule is satisfied vacuously at runtime AND enforced statically over every shipped file by guard-no-anthropic-runtime.py. These are deny-list prefixes, never runtime ids; client skills never use Anthropic models.

paid_tier_markers.note: D2 treats a model/provider carrying one of these markers in an idle window as paid-idle burn. A :cloud suffix on a heartbeat model is the classic idle-burn furnace (LP-A2).

process_manager_fields.note: pm2 jlist is filtered to name/status/pid/restarts ONLY, NEVER an env dump - a fleet review leaked live credential VALUES exactly that way, and the standing rule forbids it (spec Section 3, D1).
